+```
+
+Expected: FAIL because `services/control-api/src/app.ts` and its compiler/store dependencies do not exist yet.
+
+- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the in-memory store**
+
+```ts
+import type {
+  OpportunitySpec,
+  ReportSpec,
+  TaskSpec,
+  WorkflowSpec,
+} from '@openfons/contracts';
+
+export class InMemorySpecStore {
+  private opportunities = new Map<string, OpportunitySpec>();
+  private tasks = new Map<string, TaskSpec>();
+  private workflows = new Map<string, WorkflowSpec>();
+  private reports = new Map<string, ReportSpec>();
+
+  saveOpportunity(spec: OpportunitySpec): OpportunitySpec {
+    this.opportunities.set(spec.opportunityId, spec);
+    return spec;
+  }
+
+  getOpportunity(opportunityId: string): OpportunitySpec | undefined {
+    return this.opportunities.get(opportunityId);
+  }
+
+  saveCompilation(
+    taskSpec: TaskSpec,
+    workflowSpec: WorkflowSpec,
+    reportSpec: ReportSpec,
+  ): void {
+    this.tasks.set(taskSpec.taskId, taskSpec);
+    this.workflows.set(workflowSpec.workflowId, workflowSpec);
+    this.reports.set(reportSpec.reportId, reportSpec);
+  }
+
+  getReport(reportId: string): ReportSpec | undefined {
+    return this.reports.get(reportId);
+  }
+}
+```
+
+- [ ] **Step 4: Implement the compilers**
+
+```ts
+import type { OpportunityInput, OpportunitySpec } from '@openfons/contracts';
+import { OpportunityInputSchema } from '@openfons/contracts';
+import { createId, nowIso, slugify } from '@openfons/shared';
+
+export function buildOpportunitySpec(input: OpportunityInput): OpportunitySpec {
+  const parsed = OpportunityInputSchema.parse(input);
+  const seed = [
+    parsed.topic,
+    parsed.seedQuestion,
+    parsed.audience,
+    parsed.geo,
+    parsed.language,
+    parsed.angle,
+  ].join('|');
+
+  return {
+    ...parsed,
+    opportunityId: createId('opp', seed),
+    slug: slugify(parsed.topic),
+    status: 'draft',
+    createdAt: nowIso(),
+  };
+}
+```
+
+```ts
+import type {
+  OpportunitySpec,
+  ReportSpec,
+  TaskSpec,
+  WorkflowSpec,
+} from '@openfons/contracts';
+import { createId, nowIso } from '@openfons/shared';
+
+export function compileOpportunity(opportunitySpec: OpportunitySpec): {
+  taskSpec: TaskSpec;
+  workflowSpec: WorkflowSpec;
+  reportSpec: ReportSpec;
+} {
+  const taskSpec: TaskSpec = {
+    taskId: createId('task', opportunitySpec.opportunityId),
+    opportunityId: opportunitySpec.opportunityId,
+    goal: `Compile the first report shell for ${opportunitySpec.topic}`,
+    deliverable: 'report-web',
+    status: 'planned',
+    createdAt: nowIso(),
+  };
+
+  const workflowSpec: WorkflowSpec = {
+    workflowId: createId('wf', taskSpec.taskId),
+    taskId: taskSpec.taskId,
+    createdAt: nowIso(),
+    steps: [
+      {
+        stepId: 'step_01',
+        kind: 'compile-opportunity',
+        title: 'Compile opportunity into task and workflow specs',
+      },
+      {
+        stepId: 'step_02',
+        kind: 'render-report-shell',
+        title: 'Generate the first report shell payload',
+      },
+    ],
+  };
+
+  const reportSpec: ReportSpec = {
+    reportId: createId('report', workflowSpec.workflowId),
+    opportunityId: opportunitySpec.opportunityId,
+    title: opportunitySpec.topic,
+    slug: opportunitySpec.slug,
+    geo: opportunitySpec.geo,
+    language: opportunitySpec.language,
+    angle: opportunitySpec.angle,
+    summary: `First-pass report shell for ${opportunitySpec.topic}.`,
+    sections: [
+      {
+        sectionId: 'section_01',
+        title: 'Why this opportunity is in scope',
+        body: `This first slice proves that ${opportunitySpec.seedQuestion} can compile into a stable report shell.`,
+      },
+    ],
+    createdAt: nowIso(),
+  };
+
+  return {
+    taskSpec,
+    workflowSpec,
+    reportSpec,
+  };
+}
+```
+
+- [ ] **Step 5: Implement the Hono app and server entry point**
+
+```ts
+import { Hono } from 'hono';
+import { OpportunityInputSchema } from '@openfons/contracts';
+import { buildOpportunitySpec } from './compiler/build-opportunity-spec';
+import { compileOpportunity } from './compiler/compile-opportunity';
+import { InMemorySpecStore } from './store/in-memory-spec-store';
+
+export function buildApp(store = new InMemorySpecStore()) {
+  const app = new Hono();
+
+  app.get('/health', (context) => {
+    return context.json({ status: 'ok' });
+  });
+
+  app.post('/api/v1/opportunities', async (context) => {
+    const payload = await context.req.json();
+    const input = OpportunityInputSchema.parse(payload);
+    const opportunitySpec = buildOpportunitySpec(input);
+
+    store.saveOpportunity(opportunitySpec);
+
+    return context.json(opportunitySpec, 201);
+  });
+
+  app.post('/api/v1/opportunities/:opportunityId/compile', (context) => {
+    const opportunityId = context.req.param('opportunityId');
+    const opportunitySpec = store.getOpportunity(opportunityId);
+
+    if (!opportunitySpec) {
+      return context.json({ message: 'Opportunity not found' }, 404);
+    }
+
+    const compiled = compileOpportunity(opportunitySpec);
+    store.saveCompilation(
+      compiled.taskSpec,
+      compiled.workflowSpec,
+      compiled.reportSpec,
+    );
+
+    return context.json(
+      {
+        opportunitySpec,
+        taskSpec: compiled.taskSpec,
+        workflowSpec: compiled.workflowSpec,
+        reportSpec: compiled.reportSpec,
+      },
+      201,
+    );
+  });
+
+  app.get('/api/v1/reports/:reportId', (context) => {
+    const reportId = context.req.param('reportId');
+    const reportSpec = store.getReport(reportId);
+
+    if (!reportSpec) {
+      return context.json({ message: 'Report not found' }, 404);
+    }
+
+    return context.json(reportSpec);
+  });
+
+  return app;
+}
+```
+
+```ts
+import { serve } from '@hono/node-server';
+import { buildApp } from './app';
+
+const port = Number(process.env.PORT ?? 3010);
+const app = buildApp();
+
+serve(
+  {
+    fetch: app.fetch,
+    port,
+  },
+  () => {
+    console.log(`control-api listening on http://localhost:${port}`);
+  },
+);
+```
+

+- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the report client, view model, router, and views**
+
+```ts
+import { ReportSpecSchema, type ReportSpec } from '@openfons/contracts';
+
+const CONTROL_API_BASE_URL =
+  import.meta.env.VITE_CONTROL_API_BASE_URL ?? 'http://localhost:3010';
+
+export async function fetchReportSpec(reportId: string): Promise<ReportSpec> {
+  const response = await fetch(
+    `${CONTROL_API_BASE_URL}/api/v1/reports/${reportId}`,
+  );
+
+  if (!response.ok) {
+    throw new Error(`Failed to fetch report ${reportId}`);
+  }
+
+  const payload = await response.json();
+  return ReportSpecSchema.parse(payload);
+}
+```
+
+```ts
+import type { ReportSpec } from '@openfons/contracts';
+
+export function toReportViewModel(reportSpec: ReportSpec) {
+  return {
+    title: reportSpec.title,
+    meta: `${reportSpec.geo.toUpperCase()} · ${reportSpec.language} · ${reportSpec.angle}`,
+    summary: reportSpec.summary,
+    sections: reportSpec.sections.map((section, index) => ({
+      ...section,
+      eyebrow: `Section ${index + 1}`,
+    })),
+  };
+}
+```
+
+```tsx
+import React from 'react';
+import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client';
+import { RouterProvider } from '@tanstack/react-router';
+import { router } from './router';
+
+const rootElement = document.getElementById('root');
+
+if (!rootElement) {
+  throw new Error('Missing #root element');
+}
+
+ReactDOM.createRoot(rootElement).render(
+  <React.StrictMode>
+    <RouterProvider router={router} />
+  </React.StrictMode>,
+);
+```
+
+```tsx
+import * as React from 'react';
+import {
+  Link,
+  Outlet,
+  createRootRoute,
+  createRoute,
+  createRouter,
+} from '@tanstack/react-router';
+import { fetchReportSpec } from './lib/report-client';
+import { HomePage } from './views/HomePage';
+import { ReportPage } from './views/ReportPage';
+
+function RootLayout() {
+  return (
+    <div style={{ fontFamily: 'system-ui', margin: '0 auto', maxWidth: 960, padding: 24 }}>
+      <header style={{ marginBottom: 24 }}>
+        <h1>OpenFons Report Web</h1>
+        <nav>
+          <Link to="/">Home</Link>
+        </nav>
+      </header>
+      <Outlet />
+    </div>
+  );
+}
+
+const rootRoute = createRootRoute({
+  component: RootLayout,
+});
+
+const indexRoute = createRoute({
+  getParentRoute: () => rootRoute,
+  path: '/',
+  component: HomePage,
+});
+
+const reportRoute = createRoute({
+  getParentRoute: () => rootRoute,
+  path: '/reports/$reportId',
+  loader: ({ params }) => fetchReportSpec(params.reportId),
+  component: ReportPage,
+});
+
+const routeTree = rootRoute.addChildren([indexRoute, reportRoute]);
+
+export const router = createRouter({
+  routeTree,
+});
+
+declare module '@tanstack/react-router' {
+  interface Register {
+    router: typeof router;
+  }
+}
+```
+
+```tsx
+import { Link } from '@tanstack/react-router';
+
+export function HomePage() {
+  return (
+    <section>
+      <p>
+        Report Web renders the first OpenFons report shell produced by the
+        control-plane compile slice.
+      </p>
+      <p>
+        Create and compile an opportunity in Control Web, then open the
+        generated report link here.
+      </p>
+      <p>
+        Example route pattern: <code>/reports/&lt;reportId&gt;</code>
+      </p>
+      <Link to="/">Stay on the report home</Link>
+    </section>
+  );
+}
+```
+
+```tsx
+import { useLoaderData } from '@tanstack/react-router';
+import { toReportViewModel } from '../lib/to-report-view-model';
+
+export function ReportPage() {
+  const reportSpec = useLoaderData({ from: '/reports/$reportId' });
+  const viewModel = toReportViewModel(reportSpec);
+
+  return (
+    <article>
+      <header style={{ marginBottom: 24 }}>
+        <p style={{ color: '#667085', marginBottom: 8 }}>{viewModel.meta}</p>
+        <h2 style={{ marginBottom: 8 }}>{viewModel.title}</h2>
+        <p>{viewModel.summary}</p>
+      </header>
+
+      {viewModel.sections.map((section) => (
+        <section key={section.sectionId} style={{ marginBottom: 24 }}>
+          <p style={{ color: '#98a2b3', marginBottom: 4 }}>{section.eyebrow}</p>
+          <h3 style={{ marginBottom: 8 }}>{section.title}</h3>
+          <p>{section.body}</p>
+        </section>
+      ))}
+    </article>
+  );
+}
+```
+

+- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the request builder and API client**
+
+```ts
+import type { OpportunityInput } from '@openfons/contracts';
+
+export type OpportunityFormState = OpportunityInput;
+
+export function buildOpportunityInput(
+  formState: OpportunityFormState,
+): OpportunityInput {
+  return {
+    topic: formState.topic.trim(),
+    seedQuestion: formState.seedQuestion.trim(),
+    audience: formState.audience.trim(),
+    geo: formState.geo.trim().toLowerCase(),
+    language: formState.language.trim().toLowerCase(),
+    angle: formState.angle.trim(),
+  };
+}
+```
+
+```ts
+import type {
+  CompileOpportunityResponse,
+  OpportunityInput,
+  OpportunitySpec,
+} from '@openfons/contracts';
+import {
+  CompileOpportunityResponseSchema,
+  OpportunitySpecSchema,
+} from '@openfons/contracts';
+
+const CONTROL_API_BASE_URL =
+  import.meta.env.VITE_CONTROL_API_BASE_URL ?? 'http://localhost:3010';
+const REPORT_WEB_BASE_URL =
+  import.meta.env.VITE_REPORT_WEB_BASE_URL ?? 'http://localhost:5174';
+
+export async function createOpportunity(
+  payload: OpportunityInput,
+): Promise<OpportunitySpec> {
+  const response = await fetch(`${CONTROL_API_BASE_URL}/api/v1/opportunities`, {
+    method: 'POST',
+    headers: {
+      'content-type': 'application/json',
+    },
+    body: JSON.stringify(payload),
+  });
+
+  if (!response.ok) {
+    throw new Error('Failed to create opportunity');
+  }
+
+  return OpportunitySpecSchema.parse(await response.json());
+}
+
+export async function compileOpportunity(
+  opportunityId: string,
+): Promise<CompileOpportunityResponse> {
+  const response = await fetch(
+    `${CONTROL_API_BASE_URL}/api/v1/opportunities/${opportunityId}/compile`,
+    {
+      method: 'POST',
+    },
+  );
+
+  if (!response.ok) {
+    throw new Error(`Failed to compile opportunity ${opportunityId}`);
+  }
+
+  return CompileOpportunityResponseSchema.parse(await response.json());
+}
+
+export function buildReportUrl(reportId: string): string {
+  return `${REPORT_WEB_BASE_URL}/reports/${reportId}`;
+}
+```
+
+- [ ] **Step 4: Implement the TanStack Router app and the home page**
+
+```tsx
+import React from 'react';
+import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client';
+import { RouterProvider } from '@tanstack/react-router';
+import { router } from './router';
+
+const rootElement = document.getElementById('root');
+
+if (!rootElement) {
+  throw new Error('Missing #root element');
+}
+
+ReactDOM.createRoot(rootElement).render(
+  <React.StrictMode>
+    <RouterProvider router={router} />
+  </React.StrictMode>,
+);
+```
+
+```tsx
+import * as React from 'react';
+import {
+  Outlet,
+  createRootRoute,
+  createRoute,
+  createRouter,
+} from '@tanstack/react-router';
+import { HomePage } from './views/HomePage';
+
+function RootLayout() {
+  return (
+    <div style={{ fontFamily: 'system-ui', margin: '0 auto', maxWidth: 960, padding: 24 }}>
+      <header style={{ marginBottom: 24 }}>
+        <h1>OpenFons Control Web</h1>
+        <p>Compile the first report shell from a structured opportunity input.</p>
+      </header>
+      <Outlet />
+    </div>
+  );
+}
+
+const rootRoute = createRootRoute({
+  component: RootLayout,
+});
+
+const indexRoute = createRoute({
+  getParentRoute: () => rootRoute,
+  path: '/',
+  component: HomePage,
+});
+
+const routeTree = rootRoute.addChildren([indexRoute]);
+
+export const router = createRouter({
+  routeTree,
+});
+
+declare module '@tanstack/react-router' {
+  interface Register {
+    router: typeof router;
+  }
+}
+```
+
+```tsx
+import * as React from 'react';
+import { buildOpportunityInput } from '../lib/build-opportunity-input';
+import {
+  buildReportUrl,
+  compileOpportunity,
+  createOpportunity,
+} from '../lib/control-api-client';
+
+type CompileState = {
+  topic: string;
+  seedQuestion: string;
+  audience: string;
+  geo: string;
+  language: string;
+  angle: string;
+};
+
+const defaultFormState: CompileState = {
+  topic: 'OpenClaw deployment options',
+  seedQuestion: 'How should beginners deploy OpenClaw?',
+  audience: 'indie hackers',
+  geo: 'us',
+  language: 'en',
+  angle: 'beginner decision page',
+};
+
+export function HomePage() {
+  const [formState, setFormState] = React.useState(defaultFormState);
+  const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = React.useState(false);
+  const [errorMessage, setErrorMessage] = React.useState<string | null>(null);
+  const [result, setResult] = React.useState<{
+    opportunityId: string;
+    taskId: string;
+    workflowId: string;
+    reportId: string;
+  } | null>(null);
+
+  async function handleSubmit(event: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) {
+    event.preventDefault();
+    setIsSubmitting(true);
+    setErrorMessage(null);
+
+    try {
+      const payload = buildOpportunityInput(formState);
+      const opportunitySpec = await createOpportunity(payload);
+      const compiled = await compileOpportunity(opportunitySpec.opportunityId);
+
+      setResult({
+        opportunityId: opportunitySpec.opportunityId,
+        taskId: compiled.taskSpec.taskId,
+        workflowId: compiled.workflowSpec.workflowId,
+        reportId: compiled.reportSpec.reportId,
+      });
+    } catch (error) {
+      setErrorMessage(
+        error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unexpected compile error',
+      );
+    } finally {
+      setIsSubmitting(false);
+    }
+  }
+
+  return (
+    <section>
+      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} style={{ display: 'grid', gap: 12 }}>
+        {Object.entries(formState).map(([key, value]) => (
+          <label key={key} style={{ display: 'grid', gap: 4 }}>
+            <span>{key}</span>
+            <input
+              value={value}
+              onChange={(event) =>
+                setFormState((current) => ({
+                  ...current,
+                  [key]: event.target.value,
+                }))
+              }
+            />
+          </label>
+        ))}
+
+        <button disabled={isSubmitting} type="submit">
+          {isSubmitting ? 'Compiling...' : 'Create and compile'}
+        </button>
+      </form>
+
+      {errorMessage ? (
+        <p style={{ color: '#b42318', marginTop: 16 }}>{errorMessage}</p>
+      ) : null}
+
+      {result ? (
+        <section style={{ marginTop: 24 }}>
+          <h2>Compile result</h2>
+          <ul>
+            <li>Opportunity: {result.opportunityId}</li>
+            <li>Task: {result.taskId}</li>
+            <li>Workflow: {result.workflowId}</li>
+            <li>Report: {result.reportId}</li>
+          </ul>
+
+          <a href={buildReportUrl(result.reportId)} target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">
+            Open report shell
+          </a>
+        </section>
+      ) : null}
+    </section>
+  );
+}
+```
+

+```

+```ts
+import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
+import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs';
+import { resolve } from 'node:path';
+
+const root = resolve(import.meta.dirname, '../..');
+
+describe('local development entry points', () => {
+  it('ships the expected dev and docker files', () => {
+    expect(existsSync(resolve(root, 'scripts/dev/run-local.mjs'))).toBe(true);
+    expect(existsSync(resolve(root, 'scripts/check/run-all.mjs'))).toBe(true);
+    expect(existsSync(resolve(root, 'infra/local-dev/README.md'))).toBe(true);
+    expect(existsSync(resolve(root, 'infra/docker/control-api.Dockerfile'))).toBe(true);
+    expect(existsSync(resolve(root, 'infra/docker/docker-compose.yml'))).toBe(true);
+  });
+
+  it('documents the three default ports', () => {

+      resolve(root, 'infra/local-dev/README.md'),
+      'utf8',
+    );
+
+    expect(readme).toContain('3010');
+    expect(readme).toContain('5173');
+    expect(readme).toContain('5174');
+  });
+});

+- [ ] **Step 3: Add the scripts, config READMEs, Docker files, and root script updates**
+
+```json
+{
+  "name": "openfons",
+  "private": true,
+  "packageManager": "pnpm@10.6.0",
+  "scripts": {
+    "dev": "node scripts/dev/run-local.mjs",
+    "dev:control-api": "pnpm --filter @openfons/control-api dev",
+    "dev:control-web": "pnpm --filter @openfons/control-web dev",
+    "dev:report-web": "pnpm --filter @openfons/report-web dev",
+    "typecheck": "pnpm -r typecheck",
+    "test:contract": "pnpm exec vitest run tests/contract",
+    "test:integration": "pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration",
+    "test:smoke": "pnpm exec vitest run tests/smoke",
+    "check": "pnpm run typecheck && pnpm run test:contract && pnpm run test:integration && pnpm run test:smoke",
+    "check:all": "node scripts/check/run-all.mjs",
+    "docker:control-api": "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.yml up --build control-api"
+  },
+  "devDependencies": {
+    "@types/node": "^22.13.10",
+    "tsx": "^4.19.3",
+    "typescript": "^5.8.2",
+    "vitest": "^3.1.1"
+  }
+}
+```
+
+```md
+# Source Config
+
+This directory stores source-level configuration documents and schemas for the
+first OpenFons control-plane slice.
+```
+
+```md
+# Policy Config
+
+This directory stores policy configuration for budget, execution, and risk
+gates once the control-plane slice starts applying real execution rules.
+```
+
+```md
+# Template Config
+
+This directory stores report and workflow template assets that the control
+plane can use to compile a stable first-pass report shell.
+```
+
+```js
+import { spawn } from 'node:child_process';
+
+const command = process.platform === 'win32' ? 'pnpm.cmd' : 'pnpm';
+const jobs = [
+  { name: 'control-api', args: ['--filter', '@openfons/control-api', 'dev'] },
+  { name: 'control-web', args: ['--filter', '@openfons/control-web', 'dev'] },
+  { name: 'report-web', args: ['--filter', '@openfons/report-web', 'dev'] },
+];
+
+const children = jobs.map((job) => {
+  const child = spawn(command, job.args, {
+    stdio: 'inherit',
+    shell: false,
+  });
+
+  child.on('exit', (code) => {
+    if (code && code !== 0) {
+      process.exit(code);
+    }
+  });
+
+  return child;
+});
+
+function shutdown() {
+  for (const child of children) {
+    child.kill('SIGINT');
+  }
+}
+
+process.on('SIGINT', shutdown);
+process.on('SIGTERM', shutdown);
+```
+
+```js
+import { spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';
+
+const command = process.platform === 'win32' ? 'pnpm.cmd' : 'pnpm';
+const tasks = ['typecheck', 'test:contract', 'test:integration', 'test:smoke'];
+
+for (const task of tasks) {
+  const result = spawnSync(command, ['run', task], {
+    stdio: 'inherit',
+  });
+
+  if (result.status !== 0) {
+    process.exit(result.status ?? 1);
+  }
+}
+```
+
+```md
+# OpenFons Local Dev
+
+## Default Ports
+
+- `control-api`: `3010`
+- `control-web`: `5173`
+- `report-web`: `5174`
+
+## Install
+
+```bash
+pnpm install
+```
+
+## Run all three processes
+
+```bash
+pnpm run dev
+```
+
+## Run checks
+
+```bash
+pnpm run check:all
+```
+
+## Open the apps
+
+- Control Web: `http://localhost:5173`
+- Report Web: `http://localhost:5174`
+- Control API health: `http://localhost:3010/health`
+```
+
+```Dockerfile
+FROM node:22-bookworm-slim
+
+WORKDIR /app
+
+RUN corepack enable
+
+COPY package.json pnpm-workspace.yaml tsconfig.base.json vitest.config.ts ./
+COPY packages ./packages
+COPY services/control-api ./services/control-api
+
+RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile=false
+
+EXPOSE 3010
+
+CMD ["pnpm", "--filter", "@openfons/control-api", "dev"]
+```
+
+```yaml
+services:
+  control-api:
+    build:
+      context: ../..
+      dockerfile: infra/docker/control-api.Dockerfile
+    environment:
+      PORT: "3010"
+    ports:
+      - "3010:3010"
+```
+
+- [ ] **Step 4: Run the smoke test and the full repo check**
+
+Run:
+
+```bash
+pnpm exec vitest run tests/smoke/local-dev-files.test.ts
+pnpm run check:all
+```
+
+Expected: both commands PASS
+
+- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**
+
+```bash
+git add package.json config/sources/README.md config/policies/README.md config/templates/README.md scripts/dev/run-local.mjs scripts/check/run-all.mjs infra/local-dev/README.md infra/docker/control-api.Dockerfile infra/docker/docker-compose.yml tests/smoke/local-dev-files.test.ts
+git commit -m "feat(dev): add local dev and docker entry points"
+```
+
+## Plan Self-Review
+
+### Spec coverage
+
+- Long-term blueprint reduced to the approved first sub-project: covered by Tasks 1-7.
+- `apps / services / packages` first slice: covered by Tasks 1-6.
+- `config / tests / scripts / infra` first slice: covered by Tasks 1 and 7.
+- Minimal control-plane chain `OpportunitySpec -> TaskSpec -> WorkflowSpec -> ReportSpec`: covered by Tasks 2-4.
+- `control-web` to `report-web` handoff: covered by Tasks 5-6.
+- No heavy worker, scheduler, `ops-web`, or heavy infra work: explicitly excluded in the plan notes and Task 7 scope.
+
+### Placeholder scan
+
+- No `TODO`, `TBD`, “implement later”, or unresolved placeholders are left in the plan.
+- All code-writing steps include concrete file contents.
+- All test and verification steps include concrete commands and expected results.
+
+### Type consistency
+
+- `OpportunityInput`, `OpportunitySpec`, `TaskSpec`, `WorkflowSpec`, and `ReportSpec` names are consistent across contracts, service code, and both web apps.
+- IDs consistently use prefixes `opp_`, `task_`, `wf_`, `report_`, `topic_`, `evidence_`, `evset_`, and `artifact_`.
+- `report-web` and `control-web` both consume the same `@openfons/contracts` package instead of redefining local payload types.
*** End Patch

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## 读取顺序\n- 先读本文件。\n- 读取 `Memory/01_goals/` 中最新的目标文件。\n- 读取与该目标绑定的 `Memory/02_todos/` 最新待办文件。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/04_knowledge/`。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` 和 transcript manifest。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/03_chat_logs/` 和 `Memory/05_archive/`。\n\n## 记忆规则\n- 只有在用户明确确认需求或范围变化后，才修改当前目标。\n- 当前待办必须与当前目标保持同步。\n- 对话日志只追加，禁止重写或删除既有内容。\n- 活跃文件保持精简，过期内容移入知识库或归档。\n- 清洗后的 transcript 仅用于检索和定位；当精确表述重要时，必须回看原始 transcript。\n\n## Transcript 规则\n- 如启用 transcript 归档，按 client、project、session 分层保存。\n- 原始 transcript 同时保存在当前仓库的 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` 镜像和用户级 MemoryTree 全局归档中。\n- 当前仓库的 transcript 镜像只保存属于本项目的记录；其他项目的记录只进入全局 MemoryTree 归档。\n- 清洗后的 transcript 索引必须通过确定性代码生成，不能依赖模型重写。\n- 当前仓库的原始 transcript 上传权限：未设置。首次提交或推送原始 transcript 前必须先询问用户，并在此处记录答案。\n- 若用户未批准原始 transcript 上传，则 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` 保留在仓库中但不参与自动暂存；当前仓库只提交清洗后的 transcript 索引或 manifest。\n\n## Git 规则\n- 遵守当前仓库的分支、PR、CI、评审和发布规则。\n- 仅自动提交和推送 MemoryTree 自有变更（`Memory/**` 以及由 MemoryTree 管理的本文件），但在当前仓库未获批前，不得包含 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**`。\n- 提交标题使用 MemoryTree 专用标识。优先使用 `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`；若仓库强制其他提交规范，则使用兼容格式，例如 `docs(memorytree): <subject>`。\n- 对 MemoryTree 专用变更使用专用分支和 PR；仅在仓库规则允许时才开启自动合并。\n- 如果 diff 混入产品代码、共享策略文件、跨项目 transcript 文件或文件归属不清，暂存或推送前先询问用户。\n- 当 `auto_push` 启用时，心跳进程在提交后自动推送。若未配置 remote 或推送失败，会将告警写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`。\n\n## 心跳规则\n- Transcript 的发现、导入、清洗和推送由后台心跳进程负责，大模型不执行这些操作。\n- 大模型在交互会话中只负责写对话日志摘要、更新目标和待办。\n- 如果 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 中有待处理的通知，在会话开始时展示给用户。\n- 如果 `memorytree-daemon` 可用但当前机器未注册，询问用户偏好设置后提供运行 `memorytree-daemon install` 的选项。\n- 当用户要求查看最近的会话时，即时同步当前项目的 transcript，跨客户端（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI）定位最新会话，并生成延续摘要。\n- 所有交互提示必须使用纯文本问答，不依赖特定客户端的 UI 组件。\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) 鈥?highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** 鈥?override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** 鈥?lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you鈥攆ollow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP鈥攜ou're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept 鈮?using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" 鈫?brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" 鈫?debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.

Output:
---
name: writing-plans
description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
---

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`
- (User preferences for plan location override this default)

## Scope Check

If the spec covers multiple independent subsystems, it should have been broken into sub-project specs during brainstorming. If it wasn't, suggest breaking this into separate plans 鈥?one per subsystem. Each plan should produce working, testable software on its own.

## File Structure

Before defining tasks, map out which files will be created or modified and what each one is responsible for. This is where decomposition decisions get locked in.

- Design units with clear boundaries and well-defined interfaces. Each file should have one clear responsibility.
- You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. Prefer smaller, focused files over large ones that do too much.
- Files that change together should live together. Split by responsibility, not by technical layer.
- In existing codebases, follow established patterns. If the codebase uses large files, don't unilaterally restructure - but if a file you're modifying has grown unwieldy, including a split in the plan is reasonable.

This structure informs the task decomposition. Each task should produce self-contained changes that make sense independently.

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## No Placeholders

Every step must contain the actual content an engineer needs. These are **plan failures** 鈥?never write them:
- "TBD", "TODO", "implement later", "fill in details"
- "Add appropriate error handling" / "add validation" / "handle edge cases"
- "Write tests for the above" (without actual test code)
- "Similar to Task N" (repeat the code 鈥?the engineer may be reading tasks out of order)
- Steps that describe what to do without showing how (code blocks required for code steps)
- References to types, functions, or methods not defined in any task

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in every step 鈥?if a step changes code, show the code
- Exact commands with expected output
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Self-Review

After writing the complete plan, look at the spec with fresh eyes and check the plan against it. This is a checklist you run yourself 鈥?not a subagent dispatch.

**1. Spec coverage:** Skim each section/requirement in the spec. Can you point to a task that implements it? List any gaps.

**2. Placeholder scan:** Search your plan for red flags 鈥?any of the patterns from the "No Placeholders" section above. Fix them.

**3. Type consistency:** Do the types, method signatures, and property names you used in later tasks match what you defined in earlier tasks? A function called `clearLayers()` in Task 3 but `clearFullLayers()` in Task 7 is a bug.

If you find issues, fix them inline. No need to re-review 鈥?just fix and move on. If you find a spec requirement with no task, add the task.

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (recommended)** - I dispatch a fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Inline Execution** - Execute tasks in this session using executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review

**If Inline Execution chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:executing-plans
- Batch execution with checkpoints for review

## 鍏冧俊鎭?- 鐗堟湰: v003

## 9. 褰撳墠绂佹鎻愬墠寤虹┖澹崇殑鐩綍涓庢柟寮?

## 10. 鐩綍闂翠緷璧栬鍒?
### 10.1 鏍硅鍒?
```text
apps       -> packages
apps       -> services锛堥€氳繃 API / client / contract锛?services   -> packages
tests      -> apps / services / packages
scripts    -> config / apps / services / packages

docs / Memory / labs
  涓嶅弬涓庤繍琛屾椂浠ｇ爜渚濊禆
```

### 10.2 Apps 瑙勫垯

1. `apps/*` 鍙互渚濊禆 `packages/*`
2. `apps/*` 鍙互璋冪敤 `services/*` 鏆撮湶鍑烘潵鐨?API
3. `apps/*` 涓嶈兘鐩存帴 import `services/*` 婧愮爜
4. `control-web` 涓?`report-web` 涓嶄簰鐩稿悶涓氬姟閫昏緫

### 10.3 Services 瑙勫垯

1. `services/*` 鍙互渚濊禆 `packages/*`
2. `services/*` 涓嶈兘渚濊禆 `apps/*`
3. `services/*` 涔嬮棿涓嶅仛婧愮爜绾ч暱閾句簰鐩?import
4. 鏈嶅姟涔嬮棿鑻ヨ閫氫俊锛屽簲璧?API銆佷簨浠舵垨鏄庣‘濂戠害灞?
### 10.4 Packages 闀挎湡鍒嗗眰

```text
contracts
  鈫?domain-models
  鈫?shared
  鈫?adapters / skills / policy-engine / config-schema
  鈫?apps / services
```

### 10.5 Labs 瑙勫垯

1. `labs/` 鍙互寮曠敤姝ｅ紡浠ｇ爜鍋氬疄楠?2. 姝ｅ紡浠ｇ爜涓嶈兘渚濊禆 `labs/`
3. `labs/` 浜х墿鍙兘杞寲涓烘寮忓疄鐜帮紝涓嶈兘鐩存帴鍗囨牸涓烘寮忎緷璧?
## 11. 蹇呴』鍐欐鐨勬灦鏋勭孩绾?
1. 涓嶅厑璁?`docs / Memory / labs` 杩涘叆杩愯鏃朵緷璧栭摼
2. 涓嶅厑璁?`apps` 鐩存帴 import `services` 婧愮爜
3. 涓嶅厑璁?`services` 浜掔浉婧愮爜鑰﹀悎鎴愰暱閾?4. 涓嶅厑璁告妸鐪熷疄杩愯鏃惰瘉鎹€佹姄鍙栫粨鏋溿€佷氦浠樼墿闀挎湡濉炶繘 `data/`
5. 涓嶅厑璁告妸鏈畾杈圭晫鐨勪笟鍔￠€昏緫鎸佺画鍫嗚繘 `packages/shared`
6. 涓嶅厑璁哥粫杩囨帶鍒堕潰涓婚摼锛岀洿鎺ヤ粠 UI 鎴栬剼鏈彂璧锋湭鍙楁帶鎵ц
7. 涓嶅厑璁告妸闀挎湡钃濆浘鐩綍璇啓鎴愬綋鍓嶅緟瀹炵幇娓呭崟
8. 涓嶅厑璁稿厛閾哄ぇ閲忕┖澹崇洰褰曞啀琛ュ疄鐜?
## 12. 棣栦釜瀹炴柦瀛愰」鐩媶鍒?
闀挎湡钃濆浘纭鍚庯紝涓嶅簲鐩存帴瀵规暣寮犺摑鍥惧紑宸ワ紝鑰屽簲鍏堟媶鍑洪涓疄鏂藉瓙椤圭洰锛?
`repo-skeleton-v1 + control-plane-minimum-slice`

### 12.1 棣栧瓙椤圭洰鍙仛 4 浠朵簨

1. 绔嬭捣姝ｅ紡浠ｇ爜鍖洪鏋?2. 鍏堟妸鎺у埗闈㈡渶灏忛潤鎬佷腑闂存€佺珛浣?3. 鎵撻€氫竴涓渶灏忕旱鍒囩墖
4. 鏆傛椂涓嶇閲嶆墽琛屽眰鍜岄噸鍩虹璁炬柦

### 12.2 棣栨壒楠ㄦ灦鑼冨洿

```text
apps/control-web
apps/report-web
services/control-api
packages/contracts
packages/domain-models
packages/shared
config/{sources,policies,templates}
tests/{contract,integration,smoke}
scripts/{dev,check}
infra/{docker,local-dev}
```

### 12.3 棣栦釜鏈€灏忕旱鍒囩墖

```text
鐢ㄦ埛鍦?control-web 鎻愪氦涓€涓凡鏀跺彛涓婚
  -> control-api 鐢熸垚鎴栦繚瀛樻渶灏?OpportunitySpec
  -> 缂栬瘧涓?TaskSpec / WorkflowSpec / ReportSpec
  -> report-web 璇诲彇 ReportSpec 骞舵樉绀洪〉闈㈠３
```

### 12.4 棣栨壒鏄庣‘涓嶈拷姹?
1. 澶?worker 鐪熸墽琛?2. 娴忚鍣ㄦ姄鍙?3. 瀹屾暣 source registry
4. 澶嶆潅 scheduler
5. `ops-web`
6. `k8s / terraform`

## 13. 鎺ㄨ崘瀹炴柦椤哄簭

1. `repo-skeleton-v1`
2. `control-plane-minimum-slice`
3. `evidence-ingestion-v1`
4. `report-render-v1`
5. `source-registry + worker-runner`
6. 鍐嶆墿鍒?`ops-web / infra-heavy / advanced workers`

## 14. 鎬荤粨

杩欎唤钃濆浘鐨勬牳蹇冿紝涓嶆槸鈥滅洰褰曠敾寰楀畬鏁粹€濓紝鑰屾槸鍚屾椂瀹堜綇 3 浠朵簨锛?

      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## 读取顺序\n- 先读本文件。\n- 读取 `Memory/01_goals/` 中最新的目标文件。\n- 读取与该目标绑定的 `Memory/02_todos/` 最新待办文件。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/04_knowledge/`。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` 和 transcript manifest。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/03_chat_logs/` 和 `Memory/05_archive/`。\n\n## 记忆规则\n- 只有在用户明确确认需求或范围变化后，才修改当前目标。\n- 当前待办必须与当前目标保持同步。\n- 对话日志只追加，禁止重写或删除既有内容。\n- 活跃文件保持精简，过期内容移入知识库或归档。\n- 清洗后的 transcript 仅用于检索和定位；当精确表述重要时，必须回看原始 transcript。\n\n## Transcript 规则\n- 如启用 transcript 归档，按 client、project、session 分层保存。\n- 原始 transcript 同时保存在当前仓库的 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` 镜像和用户级 MemoryTree 全局归档中。\n- 当前仓库的 transcript 镜像只保存属于本项目的记录；其他项目的记录只进入全局 MemoryTree 归档。\n- 清洗后的 transcript 索引必须通过确定性代码生成，不能依赖模型重写。\n- 当前仓库的原始 transcript 上传权限：未设置。首次提交或推送原始 transcript 前必须先询问用户，并在此处记录答案。\n- 若用户未批准原始 transcript 上传，则 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` 保留在仓库中但不参与自动暂存；当前仓库只提交清洗后的 transcript 索引或 manifest。\n\n## Git 规则\n- 遵守当前仓库的分支、PR、CI、评审和发布规则。\n- 仅自动提交和推送 MemoryTree 自有变更（`Memory/**` 以及由 MemoryTree 管理的本文件），但在当前仓库未获批前，不得包含 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**`。\n- 提交标题使用 MemoryTree 专用标识。优先使用 `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`；若仓库强制其他提交规范，则使用兼容格式，例如 `docs(memorytree): <subject>`。\n- 对 MemoryTree 专用变更使用专用分支和 PR；仅在仓库规则允许时才开启自动合并。\n- 如果 diff 混入产品代码、共享策略文件、跨项目 transcript 文件或文件归属不清，暂存或推送前先询问用户。\n- 当 `auto_push` 启用时，心跳进程在提交后自动推送。若未配置 remote 或推送失败，会将告警写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`。\n\n## 心跳规则\n- Transcript 的发现、导入、清洗和推送由后台心跳进程负责，大模型不执行这些操作。\n- 大模型在交互会话中只负责写对话日志摘要、更新目标和待办。\n- 如果 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 中有待处理的通知，在会话开始时展示给用户。\n- 如果 `memorytree-daemon` 可用但当前机器未注册，询问用户偏好设置后提供运行 `memorytree-daemon install` 的选项。\n- 当用户要求查看最近的会话时，即时同步当前项目的 transcript，跨客户端（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI）定位最新会话，并生成延续摘要。\n- 所有交互提示必须使用纯文本问答，不依赖特定客户端的 UI 组件。\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

# Docs Phase Restructure Implementation Plan

> **History note:** This plan intentionally preserves the pre-restructure `docs/plan1 -> docs/plan2/docs/sot/docs/references` move map as execution evidence. These old paths describe the migration source at that time, not the current official filing locations.

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Reorganize planning documents so `docs/plan1` keeps Phase 1 execution material, `docs/plan2` holds Phase 2 architecture and case materials, `docs/sot` holds cross-phase source-of-truth docs, and raw input material is separated into references.

**Architecture:** Treat the docs tree as four layers: phase-specific execution, phase-specific product planning, cross-phase source of truth, and raw/reference inputs. Move files without changing their substantive content, then add lightweight index files and update any path wording that would become misleading after the move.

**Tech Stack:** Markdown, HTML, PowerShell file operations, Git working tree

---

### Task 1: Establish the target directory structure

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/plan1/README.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/README.md`
- Create: `docs/sot/README.md`
- Create: `docs/references/README.md`
- Create: `docs/references/raw-inputs/README.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the target directories**

Run:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "d:\demo1\openfons\docs\plan2"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "d:\demo1\openfons\docs\sot"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "d:\demo1\openfons\docs\references"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "d:\demo1\openfons\docs\references\raw-inputs"
```

Expected: all four directories exist after the command.

- [ ] **Step 2: Add index readmes**

Write short index files that explain:
- `docs/plan1` is Phase 1 execution and validation only.
- `docs/plan2` is Phase 2 architecture, opportunity, and case planning.
- `docs/sot` is cross-phase source-of-truth.
- `docs/references/raw-inputs` stores raw imported context, not formal plan docs.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify the structure exists**

Run:

```powershell
Get-ChildItem "d:\demo1\openfons\docs" | Select-Object Name, Mode
```

Expected: `plan1`, `plan2`, `sot`, and `references` appear in the listing.

### Task 2: Move Phase 2 planning files

**Files:**

Output:
# First Batch Page Briefs Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Create page-level launch brief and ReportSpec docs for the 6 first-batch pages across the OpenClaw and AI procurement themes.

**Architecture:** Use one folder under `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/` and create one Markdown file per page so each page has a focused launch brief, clear query ownership, evidence requirements, and a reusable ReportSpec JSON block. Keep the structure consistent across all 6 files so they can later feed content production, Next.js rendering, or worker handoff.

**Tech Stack:** Markdown documentation, existing `docs/plan2` case docs, Git

---

### Task 1: Create launch brief folder and file set

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/openclaw-deployment-options.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/openclaw-best-for-beginners.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/openclaw-windows-wsl2.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/ai-procurement-options.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/ai-cheap-coding-models.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/direct-api-vs-openrouter.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the directory**

Run:

```bash
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "d:\demo1\openfons\docs\plan2\launch-briefs"
```

Expected: directory exists

- [ ] **Step 2: Use one consistent template for all 6 files**

Each file should include these sections:

```md
# [Page Title]

> 涓婚锛?> 鎵规锛?> 椤甸潰瑙掕壊锛?> 浼樺厛绾э細

## 1. 椤甸潰瀹氫綅
## 2. 鐩爣鐢ㄦ埛涓庡競鍦?## 3. Query Ownership
## 4. 鏍稿績 Thesis
## 5. 蹇呴』璇佹槑鐨勫叧閿垽鏂?## 6. 璇佹嵁瑕佹眰
## 7. 椤甸潰缁撴瀯寤鸿
## 8. 鍟嗕笟鍖栬矾寰?## 9. 鏇存柊瑙﹀彂鍣?## 10. Launch Brief
## 11. ReportSpec
```

### Task 2: Write the 3 OpenClaw page briefs

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/openclaw-deployment-options.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/openclaw-best-for-beginners.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/openclaw-windows-wsl2.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write `openclaw-deployment-options.md`**

Must capture:

```md
- pillar / 鎬诲叆鍙ｉ〉
- primary keyword: OpenClaw deployment options
- thesis: compare local, Docker, VPS, and managed paths by user type
- role: first-batch pillar page
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write `openclaw-best-for-beginners.md`**

Must capture:

```md
- strong decision page
- primary keyword: best OpenClaw setup for beginners
- thesis: beginners should choose the simplest path, not the most flexible path
- role: first-batch conversion / recommendation page
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Write `openclaw-windows-wsl2.md`**

Must capture:

```md
- pain-point platform page
- primary keyword: OpenClaw Windows install WSL2
- thesis: most Windows users should start with WSL2 unless they have a very narrow reason not to
- role: first-batch platform pain-point page
```

### Task 3: Write the 3 AI procurement page briefs

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/ai-procurement-options.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/ai-cheap-coding-models.md`
- Create: `docs/plan2/launch-briefs/direct-api-vs-openrouter.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write `ai-procurement-options.md`**

Must capture:

```md
- pillar / 鎬诲叆鍙ｉ〉
- primary keyword: AI coding model procurement options
- thesis: procurement is about purchase path and total cost, not just a static price table
- role: first-batch pillar page
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write `ai-cheap-coding-models.md`**

Must capture:

```md
- strong commercial decision page
- primary keyword: best cheap model for coding agents
- thesis: low-cost for coding should mean usable total stack, not cheapest headline token price
- role: first-batch budget decision page
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Write `direct-api-vs-openrouter.md`**

Must capture:

```md
- high-controversy comparison page
- primary keyword: direct API vs OpenRouter
- thesis: routing convenience and direct purchase control win in different team conditions
- role: first-batch comparison page
```

### Task 4: Verify consistency and record session

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## 读取顺序\n- 先读本文件。\n- 读取 `Memory/01_goals/` 中最新的目标文件。\n- 读取与该目标绑定的 `Memory/02_todos/` 最新待办文件。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/04_knowledge/`。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` 和 transcript manifest。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/03_chat_logs/` 和 `Memory/05_archive/`。\n\n## 记忆规则\n- 只有在用户明确确认需求或范围变化后，才修改当前目标。\n- 当前待办必须与当前目标保持同步。\n- 对话日志只追加，禁止重写或删除既有内容。\n- 活跃文件保持精简，过期内容移入知识库或归档。\n- 清洗后的 transcript 仅用于检索和定位；当精确表述重要时，必须回看原始 transcript。\n\n## Transcript 规则\n- 如启用 transcript 归档，按 client、project、session 分层保存。\n- 原始 transcript 同时保存在当前仓库的 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` 镜像和用户级 MemoryTree 全局归档中。\n- 当前仓库的 transcript 镜像只保存属于本项目的记录；其他项目的记录只进入全局 MemoryTree 归档。\n- 清洗后的 transcript 索引必须通过确定性代码生成，不能依赖模型重写。\n- 当前仓库的原始 transcript 上传权限：未设置。首次提交或推送原始 transcript 前必须先询问用户，并在此处记录答案。\n- 若用户未批准原始 transcript 上传，则 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` 保留在仓库中但不参与自动暂存；当前仓库只提交清洗后的 transcript 索引或 manifest。\n\n## Git 规则\n- 遵守当前仓库的分支、PR、CI、评审和发布规则。\n- 仅自动提交和推送 MemoryTree 自有变更（`Memory/**` 以及由 MemoryTree 管理的本文件），但在当前仓库未获批前，不得包含 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**`。\n- 提交标题使用 MemoryTree 专用标识。优先使用 `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`；若仓库强制其他提交规范，则使用兼容格式，例如 `docs(memorytree): <subject>`。\n- 对 MemoryTree 专用变更使用专用分支和 PR；仅在仓库规则允许时才开启自动合并。\n- 如果 diff 混入产品代码、共享策略文件、跨项目 transcript 文件或文件归属不清，暂存或推送前先询问用户。\n- 当 `auto_push` 启用时，心跳进程在提交后自动推送。若未配置 remote 或推送失败，会将告警写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`。\n\n## 心跳规则\n- Transcript 的发现、导入、清洗和推送由后台心跳进程负责，大模型不执行这些操作。\n- 大模型在交互会话中只负责写对话日志摘要、更新目标和待办。\n- 如果 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 中有待处理的通知，在会话开始时展示给用户。\n- 如果 `memorytree-daemon` 可用但当前机器未注册，询问用户偏好设置后提供运行 `memorytree-daemon install` 的选项。\n- 当用户要求查看最近的会话时，即时同步当前项目的 transcript，跨客户端（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI）定位最新会话，并生成延续摘要。\n- 所有交互提示必须使用纯文本问答，不依赖特定客户端的 UI 组件。\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.3 seconds Output: # OpenFons OpenFons is an open-source platform for source-native collection, signal watch, opinion analysis, and intelligence workflo...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.3 seconds
Output:
# OpenFons

OpenFons is an open-source platform for source-native collection, signal watch, opinion analysis, and intelligence workflows.

It is designed around one core idea:

> Turn natural-language or UI-driven research intent into structured plans, execute them deterministically, and preserve evidence for review and delivery.

## Current Status

This repository is the Greenfield bootstrap workspace for the next-generation OpenFons platform.

The current phase focuses on:

- core architecture and planning
- contract and domain-model design
- source-native execution pipeline design
- control-plane and ops-plane bootstrap

## Core Principles

- `Agent compiles, Worker executes`
- `Topic / TopicRun / Evidence / Artifact` are first-class objects
- `TaskSpec / WorkflowSpec / ReportSpec` are the stable intermediate contracts
- Skill, Web UI, and API must converge on the same execution model
- LLM browser automation is a controlled fallback, not the default runtime

## Initial Repository Shape

```text
openfons/
  apps/
  services/
  packages/
  config/
  data/
  infra/
  docs/
  tests/
```

## Imported Planning Docs

The first planning set has been copied into [`docs/plan/`](./docs/plan/):

- `鐙珛浠撳簱Greenfield閲嶅惎涓庡懡鍚嶆柟妗?md`
- `缁熶竴椋庢帶瀵规姉骞冲彴鏋舵瀯鏂规.md`
- `plan0/缁熶竴椋庢帶瀵规姉骞冲彴-Phase0瀹炵幇绾ц璁?md`
- `鍏ㄥ钩鍙版暟鎹噰闆嗕笌鏅鸿兘鍒嗘瀽骞冲彴鏋舵瀯鏂规-v2.md`

## Immediate Next Steps

1. Initialize repository governance and community files.
2. Bootstrap `packages/contracts` and `packages/domain-models`.
3. Stand up the minimal deterministic execution chain.
4. Add the first control-plane and ops-plane surfaces.

Show input payload 
{
  "command": "$path = 'docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-27-openfons-repo-skeleton-v1.md'\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Split-Path $path) | Out-Null\n$chunk1 = @'\n# OpenFons Repo Skeleton v1 + Control Plane Minimum Slice Implementation Plan\n\n> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.\n\n**Goal:** Build the first runnable OpenFons monorepo slice: workspace scaffold, shared contracts/models, one Hono API, and two React + TanStack Router apps that prove the `Opportunity -> compile -> report` path.\n\n**Architecture:** Start with a pnpm TypeScript monorepo whose first-class shared layer is `packages/contracts`, `packages/domain-models`, and `packages/shared`. Keep runtime intentionally thin: `services/control-api` owns the in-memory orchestration edge, `apps/control-web` creates and compiles opportunities, and `apps/report-web` renders report shells from the API. Defer workers, persistent storage, ops-web, and infra-heavy deployment to later plans.\n\n**Tech Stack:** pnpm workspace, TypeScript, Vitest, Zod, Hono, React, Vite, TanStack Router, PowerShell, Docker Compose\n\n---\n\n### Task 1: Bootstrap the pnpm workspace and root toolchain\n\n**Files:**\n- Create: `package.json`\n- Create: `pnpm-workspace.yaml`\n- Create: `tsconfig.base.json`\n- Create: `vitest.config.ts`\n- Create: `tests/setup.ts`\n- Create: `.npmrc`\n- Modify: `.gitignore`\n\n- [ ] **Step 1: Create the first-batch directory scaffold**\n\nRun:\n\n```powershell\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\apps\\control-web\\src\\pages\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\apps\\report-web\\src\\pages\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\services\\control-api\\src\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\packages\\contracts\\src\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\packages\\domain-models\\src\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\packages\\shared\\src\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\config\\sources\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\config\\policies\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\config\\templates\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\tests\\contract\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\tests\\integration\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\tests\\smoke\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\scripts\\dev\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\scripts\\check\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\infra\\docker\"\nNew-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path \"d:\\demo1\\openfons\\infra\\local-dev\"\n```\n\nExpected: all `v1` directories from the approved design exist, but no extra `worker-*`, `ops-web`, or `k8s` skeletons are created.\n\n- [ ] **Step 2: Write the root workspace files**\n\n```json\n// package.json\n{\n  \"name\": \"openfons\",\n  \"private\": true,\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"packageManager\": \"pnpm@10.7.1\",\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"dev\": \"pnpm -r --parallel --filter @openfons/control-api --filter @openfons/control-web --filter @openfons/report-web dev\",\n    \"build\": \"pnpm -r build\",\n    \"lint\": \"pnpm -r lint\",\n    \"typecheck\": \"pnpm -r typecheck\",\n    \"test\": \"vitest run\",\n    \"test:watch\": \"vitest\",\n    \"check\": \"pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build\"\n  },\n  \"devDependencies\": {\n    \"@testing-library/jest-dom\": \"^6.6.3\",\n    \"@testing-library/react\": \"^16.2.0\",\n    \"@types/node\": \"^22.13.11\",\n    \"jsdom\": \"^26.0.0\",\n    \"tsx\": \"^4.19.3\",\n    \"typescript\": \"^5.8.2\",\n    \"vite\": \"^6.2.2\",\n    \"vite-tsconfig-paths\": \"^5.1.4\",\n    \"vitest\": \"^3.0.9\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n```yaml\n# pnpm-workspace.yaml\npackages:\n  - apps/*\n  - services/*\n  - packages/*\n```\n\n```json\n// tsconfig.base.json\n{\n  \"compilerOptions\": {\n    \"target\": \"ES2022\",\n    \"lib\": [\"ES2022\", \"DOM\", \"DOM.Iterable\"],\n    \"strict\": true,\n    \"skipLibCheck\": true,\n    \"esModuleInterop\": true,\n    \"allowSyntheticDefaultImports\": true,\n    \"resolveJsonModule\": true,\n    \"jsx\": \"react-jsx\",\n    \"baseUrl\": \".\",\n    \"paths\": {\n      \"@openfons/shared\": [\"packages/shared/src/index.ts\"],\n      \"@openfons/domain-models\": [\"packages/domain-models/src/index.ts\"],\n      \"@openfons/contracts\": [\"packages/contracts/src/index.ts\"]\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n```ts\n// vitest.config.ts\nimport { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';\nimport tsconfigPaths from 'vite-tsconfig-paths';\n\nexport default defineConfig({\n  plugins: [tsconfigPaths()],\n  test: {\n    setupFiles: ['./tests/setup.ts'],\n    include: ['tests/**/*.test.ts', 'tests/**/*.test.tsx'],\n    environmentMatchGlobs: [\n      ['tests/smoke/**/*.test.tsx', 'jsdom'],\n      ['tests/**/*.test.ts', 'node']\n    ]\n  }\n});\n```\n\n```ts\n// tests/setup.ts\nimport '@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest';\n```\n\n```text\n# .npmrc\nauto-install-peers=true\nshared-workspace-lockfile=true\nstrict-peer-dependencies=false\n```\n\n```gitignore\n# .gitignore\nAGENTS.md\nMemory/**\nmemory/**\nnode_modules/\ndist/\ncoverage/\n.vite/\n*.tsbuildinfo\n.env\n.env.*\n!.env.example\n__pycache__/\n*.pyc\n.DS_Store\ndata/temp/\n```\n\n- [ ] **Step 3: Install the root toolchain and create the lockfile**\n\nRun:\n\n```powershell\npnpm install\n```\n\nExpected: `pnpm-lock.yaml` is created and the root toolchain installs without workspace errors.\n\n- [ ] **Step 4: Commit the workspace bootstrap**\n\n```bash\ngit add package.json pnpm-workspace.yaml tsconfig.base.json vitest.config.ts tests/setup.ts .npmrc .gitignore pnpm-lock.yaml apps services packages config tests scripts infra\ngit commit -m \"chore(repo): bootstrap pnpm workspace\"\n```\n\n### Task 2: Add `packages/shared` utility primitives\n\n**Files:**\n- Create: `packages/shared/package.json`\n- Create: `packages/shared/tsconfig.json`\n- Create: `packages/shared/src/id.ts`\n- Create: `packages/shared/src/text.ts`\n- Create: `packages/shared/src/time.ts`\n- Create: `packages/shared/src/index.ts`\n- Test: `tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts`\n\n- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing shared-utility test**\n\n```ts\n// tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts\nimport { afterEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest';\nimport { createId, nowIso, slugify } from '@openfons/shared';\n\ndescribe('@openfons/shared', () => {\n  afterEach(() => {\n    vi.useRealTimers();\n  });\n\n  it('slugify normalizes human-readable titles', () => {\n    expect(slugify('AI Coding Model Procurement Options')).toBe(\n      'ai-coding-model-procurement-options'\n    );\n  });\n\n  it('createId prefixes generated ids', () => {\n    expect(createId('opp')).toMatch(/^opp_[a-z0-9]{8}$/);\n  });\n\n  it('nowIso returns an ISO timestamp', () => {\n    vi.useFakeTimers();\n    vi.setSystemTime(new Date('2026-03-27T12:00:00.000Z'));\n\n    expect(nowIso()).toBe('2026-03-27T12:00:00.000Z');\n  });\n});\n```\n\n- [ ] **Step 2: Run the shared-utility test and verify it fails**\n\nRun:\n\n```powershell\npnpm test -- tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts\n```\n\nExpected: FAIL because `@openfons/shared` does not exist yet.\n\n- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the shared package**\n\n```json\n// packages/shared/package.json\n{\n  \"name\": \"@openfons/shared\",\n  \"private\": true,\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"type\": \"module\",\n  \"main\": \"./dist/index.js\",\n  \"types\": \"./dist/index.d.ts\",\n  \"exports\": {\n    \".\": {\n      \"types\": \"./dist/index.d.ts\",\n      \"default\": \"./dist/index.js\"\n    }\n  },\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"build\": \"tsc -p tsconfig.json\",\n    \"lint\": \"tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit\",\n    \"typecheck\": \"tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n```json\n// packages/shared/tsconfig.json\n{\n  \"extends\": \"../../tsconfig.base.json\",\n  \"compilerOptions\": {\n    \"module\": \"NodeNext\",\n    \"moduleResolution\": \"NodeNext\",\n    \"declaration\": true,\n    \"rootDir\": \"src\",\n    \"outDir\": \"dist\"\n  },\n  \"include\": [\"src/**/*\"]\n}\n```\n\n```ts\n// packages/shared/src/id.ts\nexport const createId = (prefix: string): string => {\n  const suffix = Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10);\n  return `${prefix}_${suffix}`;\n};\n```\n\n```ts\n// packages/shared/src/text.ts\nexport const slugify = (value: string): string =>\n  value\n    .trim()\n    .toLowerCase()\n    .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')\n    .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '');\n```\n\n```ts\n// packages/shared/src/time.ts\nexport const nowIso = (): string => new Date().toISOString();\n```\n\n```ts\n// packages/shared/src/index.ts\nexport * from './id.js';\nexport * from './text.js';\nexport * from './time.js';\n```\n\n- [ ] **Step 4: Run the shared-utility test again and verify it passes**\n\nRun:\n\n```powershell\npnpm test -- tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts\npnpm --filter @openfons/shared lint\n```\n\nExpected: PASS for the contract test, then a clean TypeScript no-emit check for `@openfons/shared`.\n\n- [ ] **Step 5: Commit the shared package**\n\n```bash\ngit add packages/shared tests/contract/shared-utils.test.ts\ngit commit -m \"feat(shared): add workspace utility primitives\"\n```\n\n### Task 3: Add `packages/domain-models` for topic, evidence, and artifact shapes\n\n**Files:**\n- Create: `packages/domain-models/package.json`\n- Create: `packages/domain-models/tsconfig.json`\n- Create: `packages/domain-models/src/index.ts`\n- Test: `tests/contract/domain-models.test.ts`\n\n- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing domain-model test**\n\n```ts\n// tests/contract/domain-models.test.ts\nimport { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';\nimport { addEvidence, createEvidenceSet } from '@openfons/domain-models';\n\ndescribe('@openfons/domain-models', () => {\n  it('creates an empty evidence set for a topic', () => {\n    const evidenceSet = createEvidenceSet('topic_ai_agents');\n\n    expect(evidenceSet.topicId).toBe('topic_ai_agents');\n    expect(evidenceSet.items).toEqual([]);\n  });\n\n  it('adds evidence immutably', () => {\n    const initial = createEvidenceSet('topic_ai_agents');\n    const next = addEvidence(initial, {\n      id: 'evi_001',\n      source: 'search',\n      title: 'Best AI coding models',\n      url: 'https://example.com/models',\n      collectedAt: '2026-03-27T12:00:00.000Z',\n      summary: 'Comparison snapshot'\n    });\n\n    expect(initial.items).toHaveLength(0);\n    expect(next.items).toHaveLength(1);\n  });\n});\n```\n\n- [ ] **Step 2: Run the domain-model test and verify it fails**\n\nRun:\n\n```powershell\npnpm test -- tests/contract/domain-models.test.ts\n```\n\nExpected: FAIL because `@openfons/domain-models` does not exist yet.\n\n- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the domain-model package**\n\n```json\n// packages/domain-models/package.json\n{\n  \"name\": \"@openfons/domain-models\",\n  \"private\": true,\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"type\": \"module\",\n  \"main\": \"./dist/index.js\",\n  \"types\": \"./dist/index.d.ts\",\n  \"exports\": {\n    \".\": {\n      \"types\": \"./dist/index.d.ts\",\n      \"default\": \"./dist/index.js\"\n    }\n  },\n  \"dependencies\": {\n    \"@openfons/shared\": \"workspace:*\"\n  },\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"build\": \"tsc -p tsconfig.json\",\n    \"lint\": \"tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit\",\n    \"typecheck\": \"tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n```json\n// packages/domain-models/tsconfig.json\n{\n  \"extends\": \"../../tsconfig.base.json\",\n  \"compilerOptions\": {\n    \"module\": \"NodeNext\",\n    \"moduleResolution\": \"NodeNext\",\n    \"declaration\": true,\n    \"rootDir\": \"src\",\n    \"outDir\": \"dist\"\n  },\n  \"include\": [\"src/**/*\"]\n}\n```\n\n```ts\n// packages/domain-models/src/index.ts\nimport { createId, nowIso } from '@openfons/shared';\n\nexport type TopicStatus = 'draft' | 'ready';\nexport type ArtifactType = 'opportunity' | 'report';\n\nexport type Topic = {\n  id: string;\n  query: string;\n  market: string;\n  audience: string;\n  status: TopicStatus;\n};\n\nexport type Evidence = {\n  id: string;\n  source: string;\n  title: string;\n  url: string;\n  collectedAt: string;\n  summary: string;\n};\n\nexport type EvidenceSet = {\n  id: string;\n  topicId: string;\n  createdAt: string;\n  items: Evidence[];\n};\n\nexport type Artifact = {\n  id: string;\n  topicId: string;\n  type: ArtifactType;\n  storage: 'memory' | 'file';\n  uri: string;\n  createdAt: string;\n};\n\nexport const createEvidenceSet = (topicId: string): EvidenceSet => ({\n  id: createId('es'),\n  topicId,\n  createdAt: nowIso(),\n  items: []\n});\n\nexport const addEvidence = (\n  evidenceSet: EvidenceSet,\n  evidence: Evidence\n): EvidenceSet => ({\n  ...evidenceSet,\n  items: [...evidenceSet.items, evidence]\n});\n```\n\n- [ ] **Step 4: Run the domain-model test again and verify it passes**\n\nRun:\n\n```powershell\npnpm install\npnpm test -- tests/contract/domain-models.test.ts\npnpm --filter @openfons/domain-models lint\n```\n\nExpected: the workspace links `@openfons/shared`, the domain-model test passes, and the package typecheck is clean.\n\n- [ ] **Step 5: Commit the domain-model package**\n\n```bash\ngit add packages/domain-models tests/contract/domain-models.test.ts pnpm-lock.yaml\ngit commit -m \"feat(domain): add topic and evidence models\"\n```\n'@\nSet-Content -Encoding UTF8 $path $chunk1",
  "workdir": "d:\\demo1\\openfons",
  "timeout_ms": 10000
}

If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions.   "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## 读取顺序\n- 先读本文件。\n- 读取 `Memory/01_goals/` 中最新的目标文件。\n- 读取与该目标绑定的 `Memory/02_todos/` 最新待办文件。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/04_knowledge/`。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` 和 transcript manifest。\n- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/03_chat_logs/` 和 `Memory/05_archive/`。\n\n## 记忆规则\n- 只有在用户明确确认需求或范围变化后，才修改当前目标。\n- 当前待办必须与当前目标保持同步。\n- 对话日志只追加，禁止重写或删除既有内容。\n- 活跃文件保持精简，过期内容移入知识库或归档。\n- 清洗后的 transcript 仅用于检索和定位；当精确表述重要时，必须回看原始 transcript。\n\n## Transcript 规则\n- 如启用 transcript 归档，按 client、project、session 分层保存。\n- 原始 transcript 同时保存在当前仓库的 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` 镜像和用户级 MemoryTree 全局归档中。\n- 当前仓库的 transcript 镜像只保存属于本项目的记录；其他项目的记录只进入全局 MemoryTree 归档。\n- 清洗后的 transcript 索引必须通过确定性代码生成，不能依赖模型重写。\n- 当前仓库的原始 transcript 上传权限：未设置。首次提交或推送原始 transcript 前必须先询问用户，并在此处记录答案。\n- 若用户未批准原始 transcript 上传，则 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` 保留在仓库中但不参与自动暂存；当前仓库只提交清洗后的 transcript 索引或 manifest。\n\n## Git 规则\n- 遵守当前仓库的分支、PR、CI、评审和发布规则。\n- 仅自动提交和推送 MemoryTree 自有变更（`Memory/**` 以及由 MemoryTree 管理的本文件），但在当前仓库未获批前，不得包含 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**`。\n- 提交标题使用 MemoryTree 专用标识。优先使用 `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`；若仓库强制其他提交规范，则使用兼容格式，例如 `docs(memorytree): <subject>`。\n- 对 MemoryTree 专用变更使用专用分支和 PR；仅在仓库规则允许时才开启自动合并。\n- 如果 diff 混入产品代码、共享策略文件、跨项目 transcript 文件或文件归属不清，暂存或推送前先询问用户。\n- 当 `auto_push` 启用时，心跳进程在提交后自动推送。若未配置 remote 或推送失败，会将告警写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`。\n\n## 心跳规则\n- Transcript 的发现、导入、清洗和推送由后台心跳进程负责，大模型不执行这些操作。\n- 大模型在交互会话中只负责写对话日志摘要、更新目标和待办。\n- 如果 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 中有待处理的通知，在会话开始时展示给用户。\n- 如果 `memorytree-daemon` 可用但当前机器未注册，询问用户偏好设置后提供运行 `memorytree-daemon install` 的选项。\n- 当用户要求查看最近的会话时，即时同步当前项目的 transcript，跨客户端（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI）定位最新会话，并生成延续摘要。\n- 所有交互提示必须使用纯文本问答，不依赖特定客户端的 UI 组件。\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}
